description: Capture computer audio locally without adding a visible meeting bot, with explicit user intent and participant notice controls.
user_story: As a PM, I want Granola to capture meeting audio locally without a bot so I can keep meetings lightweight and private.
acceptance_criteria: User can start/stop capture from desktop; capture state is always visible; participant notice/consent flow is available; no recording starts without user action; audio file is retained only under configured retention policy.

description: Convert captured audio into clean transcript text suitable for downstream summary, Q&A, and artifact generation.
user_story: As a user, I want captured audio transcribed and cleaned so generated notes are readable and actionable.
acceptance_criteria: Audio is transcribed after capture; filler/noise cleanup is applied; transcript is attached to the note; transcript processing failures are shown with retry; transcript source remains accessible from generated outputs.

description: Allow users to type shorthand keywords during meetings that steer later AI expansion and document formatting.
user_story: As a PM, I want to type short notes during calls so Granola can infer what matters and generate better product artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: Editor accepts shorthand while recording; shorthand is saved with timestamps; shorthand can be edited before enhancement; shorthand influences generated summary and artifact structure; no typed note is lost on app restart.

description: Link typed shorthand notes to precise transcript/audio timestamps to preserve the core proprietary context signal.
user_story: As a reviewer, I want every generated expansion tied back to the audio moment that informed it so I can verify accuracy quickly.
acceptance_criteria: Each shorthand item stores timestamp metadata; generated note sections include source anchors; clicking a source opens transcript/audio at the relevant segment; missing/weak source links lower confidence; timestamp map is queryable by downstream workflows.

description: Provide an explicit user-triggered enhancement flow that transforms raw shorthand and transcript text into structured notes.
user_story: As a user, I want to click Enhance after a meeting so Granola generates polished notes only when I ask it to.
acceptance_criteria: Enhance action is user-initiated; progress state is visible; output can be regenerated; errors preserve original transcript and shorthand; generated output records model/provider/version metadata for evaluation.

description: Provide an editable meeting note workspace that supports transcript, AI-generated summary, action items, decisions, and user edits.
user_story: As a PM, I want a structured note editor so I can review and refine AI outputs before sharing or exporting them.
acceptance_criteria: Note contains sections for summary, decisions, action items, risks, and transcript; user edits are autosaved; AI-generated text is distinguishable; editor supports markdown; edit history is available for feedback learning.

description: Generate default meeting summaries with milestones, decisions, risks, owners, and next steps when no custom shorthand exists.
user_story: As a user, I want useful notes even when I typed little or no shorthand during the meeting.
acceptance_criteria: Default summary works without shorthand; output includes decisions and next steps when present; missing owners/dates are marked uncertain; low evidence content is highlighted; output meets golden dataset expectations.

description: Generate product artifacts such as roadmaps, user pain points, PRDs, project updates, and release notes from meeting notes.
user_story: As a PM, I want Granola to turn meetings into product artifacts so I can move from raw notes to work outputs faster.
acceptance_criteria: Template library includes PRD, roadmap, pain-point list, project update, release update, and decision log; user can choose a template before generation; output sections match template schema; templates support team-specific customization.

description: Allow users and teams to save preferred note and artifact formats such as Jira templates, PRD structures, and shorthand vocabulary.
user_story: As a team lead, I want Granola to remember our preferred formats so every meeting output matches how our team works.
acceptance_criteria: Users can create/edit/delete templates; templates can be scoped to user/team/meeting type; generated outputs follow selected template; template usage is tracked; template changes are auditable.

description: Let users choose a generation recipe or artifact type before or after enhancement.
user_story: As a user, I want to choose the output recipe so Granola generates the right artifact for the meeting context.
acceptance_criteria: Recipe selector appears in the note editor; recipes include summary, PRD, roadmap, CRM update, follow-up email, and recap; recipe choice is reflected in model prompt; user can rerun with a different recipe; recipe choices feed preference learning when approved.

description: Expose transcript and timestamp evidence behind generated notes, decisions, and action items.
user_story: As a reviewer, I want to see the transcript source for generated claims so I can verify important details.
acceptance_criteria: Generated claims expose source links; source viewer opens transcript at relevant location; source evidence can be copied; missing support triggers uncertainty badge; source links are preserved during export where destination supports them.

description: Detect and preserve correct speaker attribution in notes and action items.
user_story: As a PM, I want Granola to attribute statements to the correct person so decisions and ownership are trusted.
acceptance_criteria: Transcript segments include speaker labels; generated notes preserve speaker names when known; ambiguous attribution is flagged; users can correct speakers inline; speaker corrections feed evaluation and preference data.

description: Expand common and team-specific acronyms while preserving source evidence and user override control.
user_story: As a user, I want abbreviations expanded correctly so meeting notes are readable to broader stakeholders.
acceptance_criteria: System expands known acronyms such as CRM and PM; team glossary overrides generic expansion; uncertain expansions are highlighted; users can accept/reject expansions; acronym corrections update team vocabulary.

description: Detect language switches and either translate flagged sections or route them for user review.
user_story: As a user, I want mixed-language meetings handled explicitly so I know which translated sections need review.
acceptance_criteria: Language switches are detected; Spanish sections can be translated or flagged; translated content is labeled; users can report translation errors; translation errors feed evaluation dataset.

description: Search across prior notes, folders, and meetings only within the requesting user's permissions.
user_story: As a user, I want to ask questions across past meetings so I can recover decisions and commitments without manual search.
acceptance_criteria: Search respects folder/team/private-note permissions; results show source meetings and timestamps; denied access returns no content leakage; queries can be scoped by project/customer/date; audit events are recorded for permission failures.

description: Answer questions about decisions from prior meetings using grounded meeting-history retrieval.
user_story: As a PM, I want to ask what we decided last week so I can avoid reopening settled questions.
acceptance_criteria: Decision answers cite source note and transcript segment; unsupported answers are blocked or marked uncertain; system handles project/customer/date filters; answers never cross unauthorized folders or private notes; answer quality is covered in evals.

description: Parse notes into entities and relationships across customers, projects, teams, decisions, risks, feature requests, bugs, and action items.
user_story: As a PM, I want meeting intelligence connected across artifacts so customer requests, engineering issues, and decisions form one map.
acceptance_criteria: Entities are extracted from notes; relationships are created with source evidence; users can view related notes/items; graph supports customer/project/team filters; sensitive/private nodes inherit source permissions.

description: Generate context briefs before meetings using calendar, prior meetings, unresolved action items, and relevant project/customer history.
user_story: As a PM, I want a brief before recurring meetings so I know prior decisions, open items, and risks.
acceptance_criteria: Upcoming calendar meetings trigger optional brief; brief includes prior decisions, open action items, relevant customer/project context, and risks; all items cite sources; user can disable by meeting type; brief respects authorization boundaries.

description: Automatically link meeting content to related CRM records, support issues, engineering bugs, roadmap items, and prior decisions.
user_story: As a PM, I want Granola to connect a customer request to the related bug or roadmap item so meeting context becomes actionable.
acceptance_criteria: System suggests related items with confidence score; links include source evidence; users can accept/reject links; accepted links appear in note and downstream drafts; rejected links improve future matching.

description: Track unresolved actions across meetings and surface them in future notes, briefs, and follow-ups.
user_story: As a manager, I want open action items carried forward so commitments do not disappear after one meeting.
acceptance_criteria: Action items have status, owner, due date, and source meeting; unresolved items appear in relevant future briefs; users can mark done/dismiss; stale items are highlighted; no item is exposed outside permissions.

description: Synthesize themes across meetings, accounts, folders, and projects for product feedback and customer intelligence.
user_story: As a product leader, I want cross-meeting customer insight synthesis so repeated pain points and feature asks are visible.
acceptance_criteria: User can run synthesis across authorized scope; output groups themes, supporting meetings, frequency, and confidence; duplicate themes are merged; user can export to roadmap/CRM/project tools; sensitive content is detected before export.

description: Answer executive-level queries across the week or account, including commitments, decisions, risks, blockers, and owner follow-ups.
user_story: As an executive, I want to query the week's meetings for commitments and risks so I can manage follow-through.
acceptance_criteria: Query templates include commitments, decisions, risks, blockers, and follow-ups; output cites source notes; answers are scoped by user authorization; unanswered/unsupported claims are blocked; results can be saved as a weekly brief.

description: Classify meetings by type to select better default templates, extraction rules, and summarization style.
user_story: As a user, I want Granola to understand meeting type so outputs are tailored without manual configuration every time.
acceptance_criteria: Classifier labels common meeting types; confidence is stored; user can override label; label selects default template; classifier quality is measured in evals; label never overrides explicit user recipe choice.

feature: Action item extraction with owner and due date
description: Extract action items, owners, due dates, dependencies, and source evidence from meetings.
user_story: As a PM, I want action items extracted reliably so I can assign follow-up work without re-reading the transcript.
acceptance_criteria: Action items include task, owner, due date, confidence, and source; ambiguous owner/due date is flagged; users can edit before export; extracted actions can become tasks or follow-up messages after approval.

description: Draft follow-up emails from meeting notes without sending them automatically.
user_story: As a user, I want Granola to draft follow-up emails so I can communicate decisions and next steps faster.
acceptance_criteria: User can generate email draft from note; draft includes decisions, owners, and next steps; draft has tone controls; send is never automatic; external recipients trigger approval and sensitive-content checks.

description: Generate customer-facing or stakeholder-facing recaps with tone, redaction, approval, and source-review controls.
user_story: As a customer-facing user, I want a polished recap mode so I can share meeting outcomes safely.
acceptance_criteria: Recap mode has tone and audience controls; sensitive content is automatically flagged; user can redact sections; external share requires approval; exported recap records audit metadata.

description: Generate Salesforce and HubSpot-ready field updates, next steps, account notes, risks, and opportunities from meeting notes.
user_story: As a sales or customer-facing user, I want CRM updates drafted from meeting notes so account records stay current.
acceptance_criteria: System maps meeting outputs to CRM fields; generated updates include next steps, risks, and account notes; user approval is required before write; outcome-based update event is tracked for billing; unsupported fields are flagged.

description: Create draft tasks for Jira, Linear, Asana, Todoist, or similar systems based on extracted action items and decisions.
user_story: As a PM, I want meeting action items drafted as project tasks so work moves into the team's execution system.
acceptance_criteria: User can select destination; task draft includes title, description, owner, due date, labels, and source; write requires approval; unsupported destinations can export copyable structured text; user corrections update task-format preferences.

description: Export notes and curated recaps into Notion, Google Docs, Confluence, or equivalent knowledge-base destinations.
user_story: As a team, I want selected notes published to our knowledge base so account and project memory is shared.
acceptance_criteria: User can choose destination and page/folder; export preview is shown; permissions and external-share checks run; supported destinations receive formatted content; source links and confidence labels are preserved where possible.

description: Draft Slack or messaging updates from meeting notes while requiring user approval before sending.
user_story: As a team lead, I want Granola to draft team updates so decisions and blockers reach the right channel quickly.
acceptance_criteria: Draft can be generated from note; channel/recipient selection is explicit; sensitive content and external recipient checks run; send is never automatic; sent/dismissed draft outcome is logged.

feature: Formal PRD and roadmap document generation
description: Generate formal PRDs, roadmap updates, and project-plan drafts from one or multiple meetings.
user_story: As a PM, I want Granola to produce formal product documents from meeting context so I can ship decision-ready artifacts.
acceptance_criteria: User can select source meetings; generated document follows selected template; unsupported assumptions are highlighted; source citations are included; user can export to docs/project tools after approval.

description: Centralize pending AI-generated drafts, exports, CRM updates, and task writes for human review.
user_story: As a user, I want one review queue for AI-suggested actions so I can approve or reject downstream work safely.
acceptance_criteria: Inbox shows pending drafts and writes; each item includes source, confidence, destination, and risk flags; user can approve, edit, reject, or defer; rejected/edited items feed feedback learning; high-risk items require explicit confirmation.

description: Expose clean, human-guided meeting data to AI development tools through a Model Context Protocol server framework.
user_story: As a technical customer, I want meeting context available to tools like Cursor and Claude Code so engineering work can use PM meeting intelligence.
acceptance_criteria: MCP server exposes authorized notes, decisions, action items, and source metadata; access is scoped by user/team permissions; setup docs are available; logs capture all external context reads; framework can run without exposing private notes by default.

description: Create a common approval layer for any external share, send, create, update, or delete action.
user_story: As an enterprise admin, I want all external tool actions gated consistently so AI cannot act outside policy.
acceptance_criteria: All external writes route through approval service; service records user, destination, payload, confidence, and risk flags; policy can block actions by destination/type; approval status is visible in audit log; low-confidence actions cannot bypass approval.

description: Define and enforce granular permissions for CRM, task, docs, Slack, email, and MCP integrations.
user_story: As an admin, I want integration scopes controlled by tenant policy so Granola only accesses approved systems and records.
acceptance_criteria: Each integration declares read/write scopes; tenant policy can disable scopes; user consent is recorded; permission failures return safe errors; scope changes are audited.

feature: Local files and vector index sync
description: Keep local note files, metadata, and vector indexes portable while supporting cross-meeting search.
user_story: As a privacy-sensitive user, I want my meeting files portable and local-first while still enabling search and context retrieval.
acceptance_criteria: Notes remain exportable as local files; vector index can be rebuilt from local data; sync stores only configured metadata; portability test validates migration; user can disable cloud sync where supported.

description: Provide approved webhooks/API endpoints for meeting-created, note-enhanced, action-item-extracted, and draft-approved events.
user_story: As an enterprise developer, I want event hooks so Granola can fit into our existing workflow automation.
acceptance_criteria: Webhook events are documented; payloads include IDs, timestamps, confidence, and source links; admins can configure destinations; retries and failures are visible; PII/sensitive content policy applies before emission.

description: Partner or integrate with recording-heavy meeting library tools for video, transcript archive, or compliance workflows rather than rebuilding them.
user_story: As a team needing searchable recordings, I want Granola to connect to meeting libraries so I can keep using approved archive systems.
acceptance_criteria: Integration strategy identifies supported partner workflows; Granola can deep-link or export to partner library; compliance archive use cases are routed to partner; product does not duplicate video-library capabilities; partner permissions are enforced.

description: Capture user edits to AI-generated notes as structured signals, including deleted text, rewritten bullets, action item changes, and speaker corrections.
user_story: As a user, I want Granola to learn from corrections so future notes get closer to my preferred style and structure.
acceptance_criteria: Edit events capture before/after text, section, meeting type, timestamp, and user scope; users can disable durable learning; events exclude disallowed sensitive content; events feed preference profile and eval queue; telemetry is locally inspectable where required.

description: Give users explicit control to mark whether an edit should become a durable preference or one-off cleanup.
user_story: As a user, I want to decide what Granola learns from so personal and team preferences are intentional.
acceptance_criteria: Every meaningful edit can be marked learn/don't learn; default follows tenant policy; learned edits show the inferred preference; users can undo learned preference; sensitive/high-risk content requires explicit approval before durable learning.

description: Build durable profiles for writing style, detail level, action-item format, templates, vocabulary, and meeting-type preferences.
user_story: As a team, I want Granola to remember our style and formatting standards so every output requires less cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Preference profile stores explicit and learned preferences; profile is scoped to user/team; users/admins can inspect, edit, and reset preferences; generation uses profile by meeting type; profile use is disclosed in output metadata.

feature: Weekly eval dataset update from corrections
description: Turn approved user corrections into structured evaluation cases to improve regression coverage without training on customer content by default.
user_story: As the product team, I want production corrections converted into evals so model changes do not regress real user workflows.
acceptance_criteria: Approved corrections can create anonymized or tenant-local eval cases; weekly batch job summarizes new cases; sensitive content is excluded unless explicitly allowed; cases include input, expected output, judge type, and source category; eval set version is tracked.

description: Use anonymized aggregate patterns to improve defaults such as meeting-type templates, summarization heuristics, and action-item detection.
user_story: As an admin, I want aggregate learning to improve defaults only when privacy and contract rules allow it.
acceptance_criteria: Network learning is opt-in by tenant policy; data is anonymized/aggregated; no customer transcript content is exposed; aggregate patterns improve default templates and classifiers; admin can view and disable participation.

description: Maintain team-specific glossary for acronyms, names, product terms, customer names, and shorthand mappings.
user_story: As a team, I want Granola to know our vocabulary so notes use the right terms and expansions.
acceptance_criteria: Users can add/edit/remove glossary entries; system suggests entries from corrections; glossary can be scoped to user/team/project; glossary entries are used in transcription cleanup and generation; conflicting entries are flagged.

description: Update local prompt examples based on accepted corrections without relying on core-model training.
user_story: As a user, I want Granola to improve outputs from my corrections without sending private transcripts for model training.
acceptance_criteria: Accepted corrections can update local prompt examples; prompt changes are versioned; prompt example use is scoped to user/team; bad examples can be rolled back; prompt updates are tested against eval set before release.

description: Expand the 5-row golden dataset into a broad reliability suite covering diverse PM meeting scenarios and edge cases.
user_story: As the product team, I want a large golden dataset so we can measure progress toward the 95% accuracy target credibly.
acceptance_criteria: Dataset has at least 100 cases; includes multi-speaker, acronyms, accents, cross-talk, poor audio, no shorthand, mixed language, prior decision lookup, CRM/task generation, and adversarial cases; cases include expected output and judge type; dataset version is tracked.

description: Build automated tests that validate output structure and formatting across model and prompt changes.
user_story: As an engineer, I want automated regression tests so model changes do not break note and artifact formatting.
acceptance_criteria: Tests run in CI; tests cover each supported artifact template; failures show diff against expected structure; tests run against multiple provider/model configurations; release is blocked on critical formatting regressions.

description: Implement mixed evaluators for subjective summaries, speaker attribution, acronym expansion, source grounding, and workflow outputs.
user_story: As the AI team, I want evaluator harnesses so quality can be measured consistently across prompt and model releases.
acceptance_criteria: Harness supports LLM judges and deterministic rules; judge type is configured per eval case; evaluator outputs score and rationale; evaluator agreement is monitored; false positives can be reviewed and corrected.

description: Track accuracy, hallucination rate, latency p95, drift velocity, eval pass rate, and confidence distribution.
user_story: As a product leader, I want a reliability dashboard so I can see whether the product meets its trust contract.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard includes accuracy, hallucination rate, p95 latency, drift velocity, eval pass rate, and confidence bucket trends; metrics are filterable by model, template, meeting type, and release; alert thresholds are configurable; data is exportable.

description: Detect generated claims, commitments, decisions, or action items that lack source support.
user_story: As a reviewer, I want unsupported claims flagged so I do not share inaccurate meeting notes.
acceptance_criteria: Detector checks outputs for source support; unsupported claims are highlighted; unsupported external commitments trigger approval/escalation; grounding score contributes to confidence; false positive/negative examples feed evals.

description: Instrument end-to-end and per-stage latency for transcription, triage, frontier generation, search, export, and external tool drafts.
user_story: As an engineer, I want latency telemetry so we can protect user experience and cost as workflows scale.
acceptance_criteria: Each pipeline stage emits latency metrics; dashboard shows p50/p95/p99 by feature/model; alert thresholds can be configured; slow requests include trace IDs; latency regressions can block release.

description: Detect quality shifts after model, prompt, template, or provider changes.
user_story: As the AI team, I want drift monitoring so changes in model behavior are caught before users lose trust.
acceptance_criteria: Eval scores are compared by release/version; drift alerts fire on threshold breach; dashboard highlights affected templates and models; rollback candidate is identified; drift events are logged in release notes.

description: Assign confidence levels to generated notes, sentences, decisions, action items, and external workflow drafts.
user_story: As a user, I want confidence shown at the right level so I know what needs review before acting.
acceptance_criteria: Confidence is computed using transcript match, shorthand support, model score, source support, and risk category; confidence attaches to sections and sentences; thresholds align to UX buckets; confidence is stored for audit and analytics.

description: Display confidence UX with auto-published, synced, verify-details, and low-audio-match states.
user_story: As a user, I want uncertainty shown inline so I can focus review effort where it matters.
acceptance_criteria: High confidence uses green auto/synced state; uncertain content uses amber underline and verify badge; low confidence uses red warning box; users can toggle all AI-generated text; low confidence blocks external export until edited or rerun.

description: Block external exports or writes when confidence is too low or source support is missing.
user_story: As an enterprise user, I want risky outputs blocked before export so inaccurate content does not leave Granola.
acceptance_criteria: Content below configured threshold is blocked from export/write; user sees reason and source gaps; user can edit or rerun to resolve; override behavior follows admin policy; blocked event is audited.

feature: Capture consent and participant notice flow
description: Require visible user intent and support participant notice/consent before capture, recording, or summarization.
user_story: As an enterprise admin, I want recording notice and consent controls so the product complies with regional and company rules.
acceptance_criteria: Capture cannot auto-start; notice/consent options are configurable by tenant; consent state is stored with meeting record; missing consent can block capture/export; regional policy copy can be configured.

description: Detect legal, HR, medical, financial, security, incident, acquisition, board, compensation, and customer-confidential content.
user_story: As a user, I want sensitive topics flagged before sharing so I avoid exposing regulated or confidential material.
acceptance_criteria: Classifier flags sensitive categories; flagged content requires review before sharing/export; flags appear in note and review inbox; admin can configure category policy; false positives/negatives can be reported.

description: Ensure memory answers are restricted to meetings, folders, and notes the user is authorized to access.
user_story: As an admin, I want Q&A grounded in permissions so private notes and folders are not exposed through AI search.
acceptance_criteria: Retriever enforces permission filters before generation; answer citations only include accessible sources; failed access attempts are audited; cross-folder/team/customer access follows policy; unauthorized content never appears in prompt or output.

description: Require human approval before notes are shared with another person, team, customer, or external domain.
user_story: As a user, I want Granola to require approval before sharing so private meeting content is not exposed accidentally.
acceptance_criteria: Share action opens approval preview; external domain is detected; sensitive/confidence flags are visible; user must explicitly approve; approval is logged; tenant policy can disable external share entirely.

description: Require human approval before sending emails/messages or creating/updating CRM, support, project, or customer-facing records.
user_story: As an enterprise admin, I want all external writes approved so AI cannot alter systems or send messages without intent.
acceptance_criteria: Send/write actions require approval; preview includes payload and destination; high-risk actions are blocked or escalated; approved action records audit trail; system never sends as the user automatically.

description: Route content or actions to review when confidence drops below 70%, source support is missing, sensitive topics appear, or permissions fail.
user_story: As a user, I want risky outputs routed to review so I can trust what the system is allowed to do automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Engine evaluates confidence, source support, sensitivity, action type, recipient domain, permission status, and disputed content; trigger reason is visible; triggered items enter review inbox; policy thresholds are configurable; all escalations are audited.

description: Log permission checks, sharing events, external tool calls, export attempts, failed access attempts, blocked actions, approvals, and disputes.
user_story: As a security reviewer, I want an audit log so AI behavior and user approvals can be investigated.
acceptance_criteria: Audit events include actor, action, destination, source note, confidence, policy decision, timestamp, and payload hash; logs are searchable; exports are available to admins; retention follows tenant policy; tamper evidence is supported.

feature: Enterprise training opt-out and learning policy
description: Allow tenants to configure whether customer content can be used for durable personalization, team memory, or model-improvement loops.
user_story: As an enterprise admin, I want learning controls so customer content is used only under contract and policy.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can configure training/personalization opt-out; policy is enforced in telemetry and eval pipeline; user-facing settings explain current behavior; policy changes are audited; default is private/no training unless explicitly permitted.

feature: Data retention and explicit delete controls
description: Give users/admins explicit control over retention, deletion, merging, and permanent alteration of meeting records.
user_story: As a privacy-sensitive customer, I want explicit deletion controls so AI cannot permanently alter records without my action.
acceptance_criteria: Meeting records show retention status; users/admins can delete according to policy; AI cannot delete/merge/alter records automatically; destructive actions require explicit confirmation; deletion events are audited.

description: Provide manual and AI-assisted redaction for sensitive details before notes or recaps leave Granola.
user_story: As a user, I want to redact sensitive details before sharing so external recipients get only appropriate content.
acceptance_criteria: Sensitive spans are suggested for redaction; user can apply/undo redactions; redacted output is previewed; source note remains unchanged unless user chooses; redaction status is recorded in audit trail.

description: Do not build HR/performance-summary workflows until stronger governance, consent, and enterprise admin controls exist; monitor attempted usage.
user_story: As an admin, I want risky HR/performance use cases blocked or monitored so the product does not create regulatory exposure.
acceptance_criteria: System detects HR/performance-summary requests; unsupported use is blocked or routed to policy message; attempted use is counted without exposing content unnecessarily; admin policy can define handling; quarterly audit reviews demand.

description: Prevent AI from making pricing, legal, HR, medical, financial, security, contractual, or policy commitments.
user_story: As an enterprise user, I want Granola to block commitments it cannot make so drafts do not create legal or business risk.
acceptance_criteria: System detects commitment verbs and regulated domains; drafts are blocked or rewritten as non-committal suggestions; user sees rationale; external sending requires approval; blocked attempts are audited.

feature: Usage metering by meeting hours and workflow actions
description: Meter processed meeting hours, AI requests, frontier-model calls, CRM updates, and downstream workflow actions.
user_story: As the business owner, I want usage metering so pricing and cost controls match actual AI workload.
acceptance_criteria: Metering captures processed minutes/hours, meeting count, AI requests, model tier, external workflow actions, and CRM update events; data is tenant/user scoped; billing export is available; metering is resilient to retry duplication.

description: Apply a soft cap on baseline meeting processing and transition heavy users to enterprise or usage-based pricing.
user_story: As a business owner, I want fair-use controls so heavy usage does not destroy gross margin.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can set baseline cap by meeting count or hours; users receive in-product warnings; overage or upgrade path is shown; cap enforcement does not lose data; cap metrics are available for support and billing.

description: Route simple, medium, and complex generation tasks across cheap/local, mid-tier, and frontier models to preserve quality and margin.
user_story: As an engineer, I want model cascading so routine work is cheap and high-value synthesis gets the right model.
acceptance_criteria: Router classifies task complexity; simple cleanup uses triage model; complex synthesis/search can use frontier model; route decision is logged; quality and cost metrics are compared by route; fallback path exists for failed route.

description: Compress raw transcript and meeting context before frontier-model calls to reduce token usage while preserving high-value signal.
user_story: As an engineer, I want semantic compression so frontier-model prompts stay efficient and accurate.
acceptance_criteria: Compression extracts decisions, action items, shorthand anchors, entities, and unresolved questions; compression quality is evaluated; compressed payload includes source references; token savings are measured; system can fall back to full context when needed.

description: Run baseline text cleanup or transcription-adjacent preprocessing locally where feasible to reduce API cost and vendor dependency.
user_story: As a business owner, I want local processing for routine cleanup so margins survive provider cost increases.
acceptance_criteria: Local model handles basic cleanup tasks; system falls back safely when local processing fails; local processing quality is eval-tested; token/API savings are measured; user privacy messaging explains local processing.

description: Separate application logic from OpenAI, Anthropic, and other provider-specific prompt/window behaviors.
user_story: As an engineer, I want a provider abstraction so we can switch models without rewriting product logic.
acceptance_criteria: Provider interface supports generation, embeddings/search if needed, cost metadata, retries, and error handling; prompts are provider-normalized; eval suite runs across providers; releases can switch provider behind feature flag; no feature depends directly on a provider-specific API shape.

description: Route around provider outages or price shocks using backup engines and real-time cost balancing.
user_story: As an operator, I want failover and cost balancing so model outages or price increases do not disrupt the product.
acceptance_criteria: At least one backup provider is configured; traffic can fail over automatically or by feature flag; cost limits can steer route choices; failover quality is eval-tested; provider incidents are visible in observability.

description: Track AI COGS by user, tenant, feature, model, meeting, and workflow action.
user_story: As a product/business owner, I want cost visibility so pricing, routing, and caps can be adjusted before margin erodes.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows AI COGS per user/month, per meeting, per model tier, per feature, and per tenant; alerts fire on anomalous usage; data ties to pricing caps and billing; support can diagnose high-cost users.

description: Emit a billable event when approved CRM field updates are completed.
user_story: As the business owner, I want CRM update events tracked so outcome-based pricing can be charged accurately.
acceptance_criteria: Approved CRM writes emit billable event; duplicate retries are deduplicated; event includes tenant, destination, record type, timestamp, and success/failure; customer-visible usage report is available; disputed events can be reconciled.

description: Centralize tenant policies for consent, retention, training opt-out, external sharing, integration scopes, caps, and audit exports.
user_story: As an enterprise admin, I want one console to configure Granola policies across users and teams.
acceptance_criteria: Admin can configure consent, retention, learning, integrations, external share, write approval, usage caps, and audit export settings; settings are versioned; changes are audited; default policies are safe and private.

description: Support enterprise organization controls such as SSO, user management, role-based access, and workspace/team policies.
user_story: As an enterprise admin, I want organization management controls so Granola can be deployed safely across teams.
acceptance_criteria: SSO can be configured; users can be assigned roles; team/workspace permissions govern note access; admin can disable users; organization settings apply to new users by default.

description: Deliver a polished app shell with clear value proposition, main input area, suggested prompts, recent activity, and responsive desktop/mobile behavior.
user_story: As a new user, I want to understand the AI workflow in the first minute so I know how to capture, enhance, and act on meeting notes.
acceptance_criteria: First screen shows value proposition, meeting capture/input area, suggested prompts/recipes, and recent activity; UI supports desktop and mobile responsive layouts; loading/thinking states are visible; dark/light mode is supported.

description: Instrument a prototype trial to validate whether PMs want shorthand-to-document translation and whether raw audio converts to shared artifacts.
user_story: As the product team, I want behavioral validation data so we know whether the core bet is real before scaling engineering investment.
acceptance_criteria: Trial tracks activation, meetings captured, shorthand usage, Enhance usage, artifact generation, artifact shares/exports, retention, and qualitative feedback; dashboard reports cohort conversion; results inform kill/continue decision.

feature: Native OS audio layer technical assessment
description: Assess Apple and Microsoft native audio/transcription APIs to determine whether Granola should build on top of OS capture rather than compete with it.
user_story: As the product team, I want a technical assessment of OS capture APIs so we can reduce platform commoditization risk.
acceptance_criteria: Assessment covers macOS, Windows, Teams, and browser capture feasibility; risks to current capture architecture are documented; build-vs-partner recommendation is produced; roadmap implications are added to the product backlog.

description: Fill the empty roadmap with prioritized initiatives, metrics, and confidence for now, next, and bet horizons.
user_story: As a product leader, I want a populated three-horizon roadmap so engineering and stakeholders share the same execution sequence.
acceptance_criteria: Roadmap lists initiatives for 0-3, 3-9, and 9-18 months; each initiative has target metric and confidence; dependencies and risks are documented; board narrative aligns with backlog epics; roadmap is reviewed after product validation results.
